title: GPU utilization and where time goes
description: Reported GPU utilization can be high while the accelerator stalls on data transfer or small kernels; throughput, not the utilization number, is the truth.
body: Use when a GPU shows busy but throughput is low. The utilization metric often means 'a kernel was running', not 'the compute units were saturated' — a memory-bound or transfer-bound workload reports high utilization while stalling. Small per-request kernels and host-device copies waste cycles. Do not trust the utilization percent as efficiency. Actions: batch to enlarge kernels, keep data on-device, and profile to find memory-bound stalls. Validation signals include throughput rising when batches grow and copies shrink. Risk boundary: chasing the utilization number instead of throughput optimizes the wrong thing; measure actual requests-per-second.
